tasks.method: Use bar charts (V1) to compare churn rates across demographic categories like gender, senior citizen status, and partner status. Encode demographic categories on the x-axis and churn rates on the y-axis.
tasks.objective: Establish baseline relationships between categorical demographic factors and churn rates.

tasks.method: Create a scatter plot (V2) of churn scores against age with gender as a color encoding. Add trend lines to highlight correlations.
tasks.objective: Identify how churn risk varies across age and explore differences by gender.

tasks.method: Group ages into bins (e.g., 18-30, 31-45, 46-60, 61+) as specified in D3. Use an area chart (V6) to show changes in churn rates across age groups, segmented by satisfaction scores.
tasks.objective: Identify patterns in churn rates across age groups while exploring the moderating effect of satisfaction.

tasks.method: Use pie charts (V5) to display the proportion of churn categories for senior citizens compared to non-senior citizens and customers with dependents compared to those without.
tasks.objective: Explore whether specific churn reasons dominate for high-risk groups like senior citizens and those with dependents.

tasks.method: Create a heat map (V4) with churn rates as the colored value and demographic factors (e.g., age groups, partner status) on one axis and cities on the other axis.
tasks.objective: Identify regions or demographic intersections with unusually high churn rates.

tasks.method: Use box plots (V3) to show the distribution of churn scores within specific demographic groups (e.g., gender, partner status). Encode churn scores on the y-axis and demographic categories on the x-axis.
tasks.objective: Characterize churn risk distributions within specific demographic groups and detect outliers.

tasks.type: retrieving values | filtering | summarizing text data
tasks.method: Display a tick chart (V8) to highlight the frequency of churn reasons across gender groups. Allow interactions to filter by gender or reason to dynamically update related visualizations.
tasks.objective: Summarize the most common churn reasons for each gender and enable further exploration.

tasks.method: Combine a bar chart and line chart (V7), with churn rates represented as bars and satisfaction scores represented as lines for different cities.
tasks.objective: Highlight whether satisfaction levels correlate with churn rates across different locations.

tasks.method: Allow sorting of a bar chart (V1) to reorder demographic factors by churn rates. Overlay churn categories as segments within the bars for additional context.
tasks.objective: Explore the impact of dependents on churn rates and investigate the reasons behind their churn.

tasks.method: Enable zooming interactions (I6) within scatter plots (V2) to focus on specific age windows with high churn scores.
tasks.objective: Analyze churn risk among high-scoring segments more closely and identify deviations or sub-patterns.
